pattern: With a hard cap on the number of boxes but no cap on which boxes, filling the truck from the highest-units-per-box type down is always at least as good as any other order, since swapping a lower-value box in for a higher-value one never increases the total.
hints: You only care about total boxes loaded (bounded by truckSize) and each box's own unit value -- the box type identity beyond that doesn't matter.

Sort the box types by units-per-box in decreasing order, then greedily take as many boxes as possible from the richest type first, moving to the next type once a type is exhausted or the truck is full.

Keep a running remaining-capacity counter; for each type (highest units first) take min(remaining capacity, boxes available of that type), add units, subtract from capacity, and stop once capacity hits zero.

edge_inputs.description: statement example one, three box types
statement example two, four box types
single box type, truck fits exactly all boxes
truck size far exceeds total boxes available, takes everything
a low-count high-value type must be exhausted before dipping into a high-count low-value type
all types have equal units per box, order shouldn't matter

stress.description: random box types and truck size up to the max
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    boxTypes = [[rng.randint(1, 1000), rng.randint(1, 1000)] for _ in range(n)]
    truckSize = rng.randint(1, 1000000)
    return [boxTypes, truckSize]
